key_insight: An exact hyperfine-relaxation model shows that localized carrier spins in metal-halide perovskites relax through different nuclear channels, with holes generally more affected than electrons and lifetimes strongly tied to halogen/metal composition.
engineering_relevance: This gives a predictive route to tune spin lifetime and carrier localization in perovskite optospintronic devices by selecting lighter halogens and metal cations.
method: Adapted a muon-spin relaxation framework to carrier spin dynamics in perovskites.
Solved longitudinal spin relaxation exactly across arbitrary hyperfine correlation times.
Applied the model to time-resolved Faraday rotation data to extract localization volume and correlation time.
evidence_signal: The model was tested against time-resolved Faraday rotation data from two perovskite samples and showed that a single-exponential approximation is insufficient in the intermediate correlation regime.
watch_item: The conclusions rely on a localized-carrier hyperfine picture, so applicability may be reduced for more delocalized carriers or materials outside the studied perovskite compositions.
paper_title: Hyperfine driven spin relaxation of charge carriers in metal-halide perovskites
